role_period: Minister of Communications and Digital Economy 2019-2023
claim_summary: NCC-linked statement reported ICT contribution to GDP at 14.42 percent in Q2 2021.
attribution_note: Attribution is sector-level under ministry/regulatory ecosystem; not sole-person causation.

role_period: Minister of Communications and Digital Economy 2019-2023
claim_summary: NCC-linked statement reported broadband penetration at 40.01 percent by September 2021.
attribution_note: National telecom outcome; influenced by operators, regulator, market, and policy.

role_period: Minister of Communications and Digital Economy 2019-2023
claim_summary: Public report stated 80 million Nigerians had broadband access by December 2021.
attribution_note: Use as context unless verified against NCC statistical tables.

role_period: Minister of Communications and Digital Economy 2019-2023
claim_summary: USPF ministry-achievements page reported more than 219000 direct digital skills beneficiaries.
attribution_note: Government achievement statement; requires project-level validation for beneficiary audit.

role_period: Minister of Communications and Digital Economy 2019-2023
claim_summary: USPF ministry-achievements page reported 1667 projects/centres established and 455 ongoing.
attribution_note: Government achievement statement; centre-level coordinates not included in source page.

role_period: Minister of Communications and Digital Economy 2019-2023
claim_summary: USPF ministry-achievements page reported more than 61 million unique NIN enrolments.
attribution_note: National identity programme involved NIMC and multiple institutions.

claim_summary: NITDA article credited agency development and GDP relevance in the digital economy ecosystem.
attribution_note: Qualitative institutional context; not converted directly to a fabricated statistic.

role_period: Minister of Communications and Digital Economy 2019-2023
claim_summary: ICT sector growth during the recession period was cited in official achievements materials.
attribution_note: Macro outcome; crisis score uses evidence presence and verifiability, not causation claim.

role_period: Minister of Communications and Digital Economy 2019-2023
claim_summary: No direct electricity/water utility management role identified in the included public sources.
attribution_note: Excluded from positive score calculation except coverage penalty.

claim_summary: Daily Trust interview described KEDCO service continuity and safety priorities during COVID-19.
attribution_note: Interview source; claims are attributed to the interview and should be validated against regulator data.

claim_summary: Interview stated KEDCO maintained public service delivery during COVID-19 and customers saw improved supply.
attribution_note: Self-reported in media interview; needs operational outage/supply-hour data for stronger scoring.

claim_summary: Blueprint reported inspection of new transformers/accessories and feeder construction to improve service delivery.
attribution_note: Project activity reported in media; no independent completion audit in seed data.

claim_summary: NERC Q2 2022 report listed Kano DisCo metering performance at 22.52 percent.
attribution_note: Regulator data; entity-level metric, not solely attributable to one executive.

claim_summary: NERC Q4 2022 reported metering, billing, and interruption were major complaint categories across DisCos.
attribution_note: Industry-wide context; KEDCO-specific complaint counts should be added when extracted.

claim_summary: KEDCO role supports regional commerce through distribution service, but seed data lacks audited economic impact figures.
attribution_note: Narrative only; excluded from quantitative economic impact score.

claim_summary: Daily Trust reported Gwamna statement that staff training and retraining were part of KEDCO transformation strategy.
attribution_note: Self-reported claim; no beneficiary counts in seed evidence.

claim_summary: Reported transformer/accessory procurement and feeder construction indicate operational improvement activity.
attribution_note: Innovation score limited by absence of audited technology deployment metrics.

claim_summary: TBI Africa reported a service-delivery pledge and staff commitment message by KEDCO MD.
attribution_note: Pledge is not an outcome; scored lightly unless paired with measured delivery data.

role_period: Minister of Transportation 2023-present / Senator for Gombe North
indicator: Official public profile and office history
claim_summary: The Federal Ministry of Transportation profile records Alkali's background, Gombe State executive roles, senatorial service, and appointment as Minister of Transportation on 16 August 2023.
attribution_note: Office-history evidence; not a performance outcome by itself.

claim_summary: The ministry reported that Alkali signed a performance bond with directors and agency heads to cascade KPIs and annual appraisal for the transportation sector.
attribution_note: Accountability mechanism evidence; implementation outcomes should be monitored over time.

claim_summary: NITT reported Alkali's position that transport can sustain the economy, reduce unemployment, lower mobility costs, and reduce movement delays for goods and services.
attribution_note: Policy/economic rationale; not a measured GDP statistic.

claim_summary: The transportation ministry reported Alkali's inspection and assurance on the first 387km Kano-Daura segment of the Kano-Maradi rail project.
attribution_note: Project target and inspection evidence; completion must be verified separately.

claim_summary: The ministry stated Alkali inspected Kano-Maradi and Kaduna-Kano rail works with engineers, consultants, and contractors for on-ground assessment.
attribution_note: Inspection and project oversight evidence.

claim_summary: The ministry reported plans to restore freight on the Lagos-Kano narrow gauge to support cargo movement to Kano's Dala Inland Dry Port.
attribution_note: Economic logistics impact is plausible but should be measured through freight-volume data.

claim_summary: Federal information reporting stated that the first National Land Transport Policy had been validated by stakeholders and approved by the Federal Executive Council.
attribution_note: Major policy milestone; full impact depends on implementation.

indicator: Smart mobility and logistics technology agenda
claim_summary: Government information reporting linked Alkali's agenda to smart mobility, intelligent transport systems, blockchain-enabled logistics, NITT logistics-drone testing, and rail unbundling.
attribution_note: Innovation agenda evidence; measurable deployment metrics should be added as they become available.

claim_summary: The performance bond included the Nigerian Railway Corporation and Nigerian Institute of Transport Technology among agencies aligning roles, KPIs, manpower, research, and rail development programmes.
attribution_note: Public transport institutional management evidence; agency outcomes are multi-actor.

claim_summary: Punch reported Alkali criticized the Port Harcourt-Maiduguri contractor after stating work around Port Harcourt-Aba was below expected pace and about 45-47 percent in the inspected section.
attribution_note: Crisis/implementation response evidence; figure is from reported ministerial inspection comments.

indicator: Public response to railway diversion allegation
claim_summary: Punch reported the ministry's denial of allegations that a $3bn railway project was diverted to Gombe, and its explanation of project sections and funding constraints.
attribution_note: Represents public controversy and official response; allegation is not treated as proven.

indicator: Transport ministry capital budget release constraint
claim_summary: TheCable reported Alkali saying only about 1 percent of the ministry's 2025 capital budget was released, while 70 percent of projects rolled into 2026 due to funding constraints.
attribution_note: Negative context/constraint, not a personal performance failure.

claim_summary: TheCable reported a legislative committee statement that the ministry secured private-sector funding commitments of about $200m despite limited capital releases.
attribution_note: Attributed to legislative committee comment in reported budget hearing.

indicator: Kaduna-Kano rail financing and port rail links
claim_summary: Independent reported Alkali's statement that financing had been secured for Kaduna-Kano standard gauge and that Badagry, TinCan, Apapa, Lekki, Ijebu Ode and Kajola rail links were initiated.
attribution_note: Media report; should be validated against financing agreements and project budgets.

indicator: Transport manpower and research mandate through NITT
claim_summary: The ministry reported NITT commitments under the performance bond to provide required manpower, innovation, and transport-sector research.
attribution_note: Institutional capacity-building evidence; no beneficiary count in seed data.

claim_summary: A public legislative guide includes Senate committee references involving FERMA and Alkali; direct FERMA agency executive leadership was not verified in the seed public evidence.
attribution_note: Kept as a research flag because direct FERMA leadership should not be asserted without stronger official source.
